metadata.title: The mRNA Vaccine Mechanism
metadata.thesis: mRNA vaccines bypass traditional dead-virus immunology by directly reprogramming human cells to act as temporary bio-factories, manufacturing harmless viral proteins that trigger the immune system's threat-recognition cascade.
governing_assumptions: The immune system learns to fight threats by recognizing foreign protein shapes (antigens).
Ribosomes inside human cells blindly manufacture whatever protein sequence the messenger RNA tells them to build.

backbone.principle: Genetic Translation: Information stored in RNA is translated into physical proteins by ribosomes, acting as the fundamental execution engine of all biological factories.

subnodes.label: Lipid Nanoparticles (LNPs)
subnodes.mechanism: Because bare mRNA is rapidly destroyed by the body's defensive enzymes and cannot cross cell walls, it is specifically encased in a microscopic fat bubble (the LNP). This lipid layer is structurally identical to the human cell membrane, allowing it to easily slip through and fuse with the cell to safely deposit the fragile mRNA instructions inside.

subnodes.label: Ribosomal Translation (Spike Proteins)
subnodes.mechanism: Once the mRNA is inside the cell fluid, human ribosomes read the strand like a blueprint and assemble the viral 'spike protein'. Because the mRNA only contains the code for the outer spike—and not the rest of the virus—the cell produces harmless spiky shapes that cannot replicate or cause disease.

subnodes.label: Antigen Presentation & Antibody Creation
subnodes.mechanism: After manufacturing the spike proteins, the cell pushes them to its surface where passing immune cells (T-Cells and B-cells) detect the unfamiliar shape. The B-cells rapidly design and mass-produce specific antibodies (Y-shaped proteins) that perfectly lock onto that exact spike shape, neutralizing future invasions before infection takes hold.

learning_prerequisites.rationale: Before understanding how cells manufacture the protein, one must understand how the instructions bypass cellular defenses in the first place.
